policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Theme Studio discovery and integration surfaces in issue #395. A clean local Node 22/V8 coverage run on 2026-07-21 passed all 1619 tests over the complete 2289-line core scope and measured 1964 covered lines (85.802%). Relative to the prior 1963/2288 baseline, the legitimate one-line scope increase lets modal accessibility semantics live on an inner dialog while the overlay retains focus-trap ownership; the new test covers that split backdrop/dialog contract. Covered lines advance by one and total core coverage improves from 85.795% to 85.802%. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Theme Studio safe sharing, curated gallery, and advanced declaration foundations in issue #396. Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet runs on 2026-07-21 each measured 21093/29523 lines (71.446%) after passing all 1862 tests; two subsequent final runs over the identical production scope each passed the expanded 1866-test suite and measured 21092/29523 (71.443%). The reviewed baseline retains the higher repeated measurement so that coverage cannot regress. Relative to the prior 21032/29463 baseline, the legitimate 60-line scope increase narrows remote-resource and executable-event recognition to real token boundaries and the complete reviewed browser event-handler set so schema-valid exported names round-trip without weakening unsafe import rejection; focused controller theories cover every newly instrumented production line and the current standardized handler additions. The unchanged four-line low-side tolerance covers the observed one-line instrumentation variance without widening it, while the required floor improves from 71.371% to 71.432%; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
